lede: A look at October 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Yoon Do-young and Bryn Evans (rugby union, born 1984), and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Hurricane Melissa
events.desc: Hurricane Melissa makes landfall near Black River, Jamaica, killing over 30 people, as well as tying the 1935 Labor Day hurricane as the most intense landfall in the North Atlantic.

events.title: Aloha IslandAir Flight 1712
events.desc: Aloha Island Air Flight 1712, a Twin Otter 300, crashed into terrain at night in Hawaii killing all 20 occupants onboard.

events.title: Prospero (spacecraft)
events.desc: Prospero becomes the only British satellite to be launched by a British rocket.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Greece rejects Italy's ultimatum. Italy invades Greece through Albania a few hours later.

events.title: Volstead Act
events.desc: The U.S. Congress passes the Volstead Act over President Woodrow Wilson's veto, paving the way for Prohibition to begin the following January.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British troops attack and capture Chatterton Hill from the Continental Army.

events.title: 1707 Hōei earthquake
events.desc: The 1707 Hōei earthquake causes more than 5,000 deaths in Japan.

events.title: Treaty of Ripon
events.desc: The Treaty of Ripon is signed, ending the hostilities of the Second Bishops' War.

events.title: French Wars of Religion
events.desc: French Wars of Religion: The Siege of La Rochelle ends with the surrender of the Huguenots after fourteen months.

events.title: Ladislaus the Posthumous
events.desc: Ladislaus the Posthumous is crowned king of Bohemia in Prague.

births.name: Graham Bond
births.line: English keyboard player, singer, and saxophonist (died 1974)

deaths.text: Liang Congjie, Chinese historian and activist, founded Friends of Nature
Thomas Graham Brown, Scottish mountaineer and physiologist
Marie Roch Louis Reybaud, French economist and politician
Jean Desmarets, French author, poet, and playwright

observances.text: Christian feast day: Abgar V of Edessa (Eastern Orthodox Church)
Christian feast day: Fidelis of Como (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Job of Pochayiv (repose) (Eastern Orthodox Church)
